welcome.title: add a subscription

desc.after: ), the app will fetch the server list and ping each one.

welcome.dropHint: or just drop a link from your browser here

leakTest.title: leak test
leakTest.noIp: couldn't get public ip — both services (cloudflare, ipwho.is) unreachable
leakTest.dnsLeakMessage: resolver ({{resolver}}) matches public ip — dns doesn't go through vpn
leakTest.ipv6LeakMessage:
v6-address ({{addr}}) visible directly — tunnel doesn't cover v6.
enable kill switch or disable ipv6 on the adapter

floating.pickServer: pick a server in the main window

subMeta.menuEngineAutoHint: from subscription header or system default
subMeta.deleteConfirmMessage: the link, servers and saved server selection will be removed. you can paste a new URL anytime.
subMeta.mihomoActivateHint: tap to activate this subscription and see groups

crashDetected.title: previous crash detected
crashDetected.message: {{count}} crash-dump(s) found this week. Settings → system → diagnostics lets you bundle a zip for support

update.upToDate: you are on the latest version

subscriptionDeleted.title: subscription deleted
subscriptionDeleted.message: paste a new URL in Welcome or Settings

subscriptionDuplicate.title: subscription already added
subscriptionDuplicate.message: this URL is already in the list

vpnConflict.title: VPN conflict
vpnConflict.message:
active VPN on interface: {{list}}.
Disconnect it before connecting.

error.title: Connection failure
error.body: VPN stopped due to an error. Open the app for details.

mergeSubs.title: merge multiple subscriptions
mergeSubs.desc: add 2-5 subscriptions in parallel, all servers in one list tagged by source

autoFailover.title: auto-failover
autoFailover.desc: if the active server pings >3000ms, auto-switch to the next one in the list

windowsHello.title: windows hello on launch
windowsHello.desc: require auth (face/pin/fingerprint) when the app starts. handy on shared machines

wfpPerApp.title: WFP per-app routing
wfpPerApp.desc: per-process rules for both engines via the Windows Filtering Platform kernel driver. an alternative to Mihomo PROCESS-NAME

autoUpdate.title: app auto-update
autoUpdate.desc: client checks for new versions, downloads the signed installer and updates itself — no need to visit a website

history.title: session history
history.desc: local connect/disconnect log: time, server, mode, duration, disconnect reason

speedTest.title: speed test through VPN
speedTest.desc: measure throughput via cloudflare CDN. optional weekly auto-test on every server

autoRefresh.title: auto-refresh
autoRefresh.label: refresh subscription
autoRefresh.hint: in the background at the chosen interval

sendHwid.label: send HWID
sendHwid.hint: sent in the x-hwid header · the server registers your device with the subscription

userAgent.label: user agent
userAgent.hint: automatic: Happ/2.7.0 for sing-box (Marzban-style xray-json with built-in routing — we convert it to sing-box JSON), clash-verge for Mihomo (clash YAML directly). if you set it manually it stays as-is for both engines.

hwid.title: device hwid
hwid.hint: windows machineguid · sent automatically with every subscription request
hwid.overrideActive: override active — the app sends «{{value}}…» instead of the system hwid
hwid.placeholder: leave blank to use the system hwid

refreshOnOpen.label: refresh subscription
refreshOnOpen.hint: fetch the server list on every launch

connectOnOpen.label: auto-connect
connectOnOpen.hint: connect to the selected server on launch

engine.title: vpn core
engine.label: engine
engine.hint: sing-box — modern engine with fast startup and built-in TUN. best for vless/vmess/trojan/ss/hy2/tuic/wireguard. mihomo — needed for anytls / mieru, XHTTP servers, and per-process routing (app rules)

appRules.title: app rules
appRules.singboxWarning: sing-box is active — app rules only work with the mihomo engine (via its native PROCESS-NAME matcher). switch the engine in Settings → engine to enable rules.
appRules.tunWarning: in TUN mode app rules only work when the subscription delivers a full mihomo-config (server type mihomo-profile) — there Mihomo owns the TUN adapter directly and sees real processes. for URI servers (vless/vmess/trojan/hy2/...) in TUN, rules are not applied — switch to proxy mode.
appRules.intro: rules of the form «<exe> → action» are applied by Mihomo to processes by their executable file name. for example, send telegram through the VPN while routing steam direct. the exe name is taken from task manager (telegram.exe, steam.exe). proxy mode only

allowLan.label: allow LAN connections
allowLan.hint: inbound listens on 0.0.0.0 — other devices on the network can use this proxy (login/password are shown after connect)

tunMasking.label: TUN name masking
tunMasking.hint: the adapter is named like a regular network interface (wlan99 / Local Area Connection / Ethernet). protects against spy apps that detect VPN by adapter name via GetAdaptersAddresses

tunOnlyStrict.label: TUN-only mode (strict)
tunOnlyStrict.hint: hide the proxy/tun switch on the main screen and operate via the TUN adapter only. no local SOCKS5 socket on loopback — for paranoiacs who refuse to leave any VPN surface on 127.0.0.1

antiDpi.title: anti-dpi
antiDpi.mihomoWarning: mihomo is active — fragmentation and noises are not applied (anti-DPI is implemented for sing-box only). doh-resolve still works via mihomo's dns.
antiDpi.singboxInfo: in sing-box fragmentation works as a simple ON/OFF — fine-grained parameters (size/sleep/interval) are not accepted by sing-box. noises are not supported in upstream sing-box and are ignored. for fine-tuning use Mihomo (anti-DPI is not implemented there yet).

fragmentation.label: tcp fragmentation
fragmentation.hint: splits the tls clienthello into chunks — bypasses most dpi

noises.label: noise packets
noises.hint: fake udp packets to confuse dpi

dohResolve.label: doh server resolve
dohResolve.hint: the vpn server address is resolved via doh, bypassing system dns (helps with dns blocks)

main.label: block traffic when vpn drops
main.hint: leak protection at the windows kernel level (wfp). works even if the vpn engine crashes. triple orphan-filter protection: dynamic-session + heartbeat-watchdog (60s) + cleanup on helper-service start

strict.label: strict mode
strict.hint: even sing-box/mihomo itself is allowed to talk only to the vpn server. direct routes (e.g. geosite:ru → DIRECT from your config) are blocked. for those who want everything through vpn, no exceptions. ⚠️ ru sites in split-routing will stop opening. requires kill-switch enabled

dnsLeak.label: block direct dns queries
dnsLeak.hint: all :53/udp+tcp except vpn-dns is blocked. protects against dns leaks. ⚠️ in proxy mode this can break apps that use system dns outside the proxy (some games, messengers). better used in tun mode

recover.label: recover network
recover.hint: if the internet feels half-broken after a crash / disconnect — one button removes wfp filters, orphan tun adapters, half-default routes and the system proxy. safe to press any time vpn is not active

diagnostics.label: export diagnostics
diagnostics.hint: saves a zip with sing-box/mihomo logs, app version, current state and the list of running vpn processes to the Documents folder. no telemetry — fully local, you decide who to send the file to if you need help

leakTest.title: leak test
leakTest.description: runs two requests in parallel: cloudflare cdn-trace for public ip + country (with ipwho.is fallback for city) and cloudflare doh whoami.cloudflare for the dns resolver ip. if the resolver ip matches your public one, dns queries are visible as your own (leak).

auto.label: auto-check after connect
auto.hint: show a toast with the real ip ~1.5s after connect

run.label: check now
run.hint: run the test manually — the result appears in the bottom-right corner

routing.intro: routing profiles decide which domains/IPs go through the VPN, which go direct, and which are blocked. rules are applied to sing-box and Mihomo on connect. for xray-json / sing-box-json configs from the subscription (with their own routing) — they are NOT applied (subscription wins)

autoTemplate.title: auto-template
autoTemplate.label: «minimal RU rules» when no profile is selected
autoTemplate.hint: when no routing profile is active — a built-in template is applied: geosite:ru + geoip:ru + LAN → DIRECT, ads (geosite:category-ads-all) → BLOCK, everything else → PROXY. useful out of the box without importing external rules

preset.title: preset
preset.label: ready-made style
preset.hint: a unique palette, background and button style at once

buttonStyle.label: button style
buttonStyle.hint: look of the main connect button

floating.title: floating window
floating.label: mini window on top of everything
floating.hint: vpn status and current ↑/↓ speed in a small window. click the dot — toggle, double-click the window — open the main one

autostart.title: autostart
autostart.label: launch with the system
autostart.hint: the app starts automatically on windows login (via task scheduler, no UAC)

notifications.title: notifications
notifications.label: native windows toasts
notifications.hint: show popups in action center when the window is hidden (connect, disconnect, error, update). in a visible window — in-app toasts as usual

shortcuts.title: hotkeys
shortcuts.intro: work globally, even when the nemefisto window is hidden. click a field, press the desired combo (at least one modifier: ctrl/alt/shift/win). esc — cancel, backspace — clear.

toggleVpn.label: toggle vpn on / off
toggleVpn.hint: same as pressing the main button — connect or disconnect

showHide.label: show / hide window
showHide.hint: same as clicking the system tray icon

switchMode.label: switch mode
switchMode.hint: proxy ↔ tun. only takes effect when vpn is stopped

trustedWifi.title: trusted wi-fi
trustedWifi.intro: on a trusted network the vpn turns off automatically. when you go back to a regular one it can turn back on (if the option below is enabled). works for wi-fi only (by ssid from netsh); ethernet/mobile are treated as regular networks.

action.label: on connect to trusted
action.hint: what to do once we join one of the networks above

autoLeave.label: auto-enable on leave
autoLeave.hint: when leaving a trusted network — reconnect, but only if vpn was disabled by this rule

backup.title: settings backup
backup.intro: exports all settings + subscription URL into a JSON file (HWID and other machine-specific data are excluded). on import a preview of changes is shown first.

urlSchemes.title: url schemes
urlSchemes.intro: the app responds to links with the nemefisto:// prefix. a bot can send such links to add a subscription or toggle the tunnel automatically.

auto.label: auto-check
auto.hint: asks GitHub every 6 hours. on a hit, a modal offers to update

about.title: about
about.privacyNote: nemefisto does not collect telemetry, does not send crash reports «home» and has no remote-control mechanisms. all logs are local to this computer. see PRIVACY.md for details.

reset.title: reset
reset.confirmSettings: settings will return to defaults (theme, anti-DPI, engine, app rules). the subscription and hwid are kept. continue?
reset.confirmAll: this deletes absolutely everything: subscription, hwid override, all settings, dismissed announcements. it will disconnect the tunnel and reload the app

crashRecovery.title: leftovers from a previous session detected
crashRecovery.intro: the previous run did not exit cleanly (crash or forced shutdown). we found:
crashRecovery.explainBase: «fix everything» — removes any of our wfp filters, deletes orphan tun adapters and force-clears the system proxy.
crashRecovery.explainRestoreProxy: «restore proxy» — reverts the registry to the original values from before the connection.

findings.proxy: system proxy is pointing to us
findings.backup: a backup of the original proxy settings is saved
findings.tun: tun adapter `nemefisto-*` is in the system
findings.wfp: kill switch wfp filters survived from the previous session

backup.title: import settings
backup.noChanges: nothing will change — every imported value already matches the current one.
backup.subscriptionUpdatedSuffix: subscription URL updated — press ↻ to reload servers

step1.title: welcome to nemefisto
step1.body: VPN client running on two cores (xray + mihomo) with protection against DPI, leaks and local detection. fully open source, no telemetry.
step1.footnote: a quick 3-step tour — takes about a minute.

step2.title: 1 · add a subscription
step2.bodyBefore: get a subscription URL from your VPN provider and paste it on the main screen. format:
step2.footnote: Marzban, 3x-ui, sing-box, base64 lists, Mihomo YAML are supported — everything modern panels return.

step3.title: 2 · pick a server and connect
step3.body: the app will fetch the server list and ping each one. tap the dropdown below the power button and pick any — latency is shown next to the name.
step3.footnote: the big round button is connect / disconnect. connecting usually takes 1-2 seconds.

step4.title: 3 · all set
step4.body: settings are in the top-right corner: kill switch, anti-DPI, themes, country routing, trusted wi-fi networks.

routingProfiles.title: routing profiles
routingProfiles.intro: import split-routing rules. format — Marzban-style JSON (DirectSites/DirectIp/ProxySites/BlockSites + Geoipurl/Geositeurl). one profile is active — its rules are applied to Xray and Mihomo on connect. autorouting is refreshed on a schedule from a URL source.
routingProfiles.emptyHint: empty. add a profile below or import via deep-link

deactivate.label: disable profile
deactivate.hint: deactivate the active one (rules will not apply on connect)

routingProfiles.addPlaceholder:
URL (autorouting) or base64/JSON (static)

URL example: https://example.com/routing.json
base64 example: eyJOYW1lIjoiVGVzdCJ9

geofiles.title: geofiles (geoip/geosite .dat)
geofiles.intro: v2ray-rules-dat files (Loyalsoldier) are cached locally. URLs come from the active profile (Geoipurl / Geositeurl). on refresh the .sha256 is fetched first — if the hash is unchanged, the .dat is skipped (saves 5-15 MB each time).
geofiles.refreshHint: download fresh .dat files if the sha256 changed. URLs come from the active profile

toast.geofilesNoUrls: no geofile URLs in the active profile

mihomoGroups.testTitle: run a delay test on every node in the group
mihomoGroups.cardTitlePreferredSelector: save as preferred — will apply on connect

proxiesPanel.title: proxy groups (mihomo)
proxiesPanel.emptyState: no groups in the current profile. connect to a mihomo profile from your subscription to see proxy groups.
proxiesPanel.staticHint: click a node → applies on connect. latency and «test» appear after VPN starts.
proxiesPanel.nodeTitlePreferredSelector: save as preferred — will apply on connect
